_comment: Model-policy artifact for the DTS (Docker Compose) deployment target.
The DTS deployment calls a direct model provider (OpenRouter, an
OpenAI-compatible API) instead of AWS Bedrock. This file is the DTS
counterpart of bedrock-us-east-1.json: it pins the model matrix and the
per-token pricing used by the spend model. It is NOT scanned by the
Bedrock-access CI gate (that gate targets bedrock-us-east-1.json only) —
OpenRouter model IDs use the provider/model form (e.g. anthropic/...)
and the single-region-native-inference residency check does not apply.
There is no AWS regional pricing premium here: OpenRouter pricing is
flat/all-in per token.
Model matrix parity (issue #269): this file's primary/critic model_ids
must agree with model-policy/bedrock-us-east-1.json on role structure
and model family/generation per role (Opus-class primary, Sonnet-class
critic — see README.md's pinned matrix), allowing only provider-specific
ID syntax to differ (e.g. "anthropic.claude-opus-4-8" vs
"anthropic/claude-opus-4.8"). tests/lint-model-policy-consistency.py
is the CI gate that enforces this; keep both files in lockstep.
SOURCE NOTE (issue #269, verified 2026-07-13 against
https://openrouter.ai/anthropic/claude-opus-4.8 and
https://openrouter.ai/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6): model_id values and
cost_per_million_* rates below reflect OpenRouter's current listing for
Claude Opus 4.8 ($5.00 / $25.00 per million input/output tokens) and
Claude Sonnet 4.6 ($3.00 / $15.00 per million input/output tokens).
Re-verify against https://openrouter.ai/models before production use if
this file has aged past the pinned matrix's last update.
The model IDs may also be overridden per-deployment via the env vars
OPENROUTER_PRIMARY_MODEL_ID / OPENROUTER_CRITIC_MODEL_ID — an override
is honored by OpenRouterModelClient.invoke() but logged as an explicit
override (backend/src/model_client.py::enforce_openrouter_policy_model_id).
